sn15.3:1.3: “From an inconceivable beginning comes the wandering-on.
sn15.3:1.4: A beginning point is not discernible, though beings hindered by ignorance and fettered by craving are transmigrating & wandering on.
sn15.3:1.5: What do you think, monks? Which is greater, the tears you have shed while transmigrating & wandering this long, long time—crying & weeping from being joined with what is displeasing, being separated from what is pleasing—or the water in the four great oceans?”
sn15.3:2.1: “As we understand the Dhamma taught to us by the Blessed One, this is the greater: the tears we have shed while transmigrating & wandering this long, long time—crying & weeping from being joined with what is displeasing, being separated from what is pleasing—not the water in the four great oceans.”
sn15.3:2.2: “Excellent, monks. Excellent. It is excellent that you thus understand the Dhamma taught by me. “This is the greater: the tears you have shed while transmigrating & wandering this long, long time—crying & weeping from being joined with what is displeasing, being separated from what is pleasing—not the water in the four great oceans.
sn15.3:2.3: “Long have you (repeatedly) experienced the death of a mother.
sn15.3:2.4: The tears you have shed over the death of a mother while transmigrating & wandering this long, long time—crying & weeping from being joined with what is displeasing, being separated from what is pleasing—are greater than the water in the four great oceans.
sn15.3:2.5: “Long have you (repeatedly) experienced the death of a father…
sn15.3:2.12: loss with regard to disease. The tears you have shed over loss with regard to disease while transmigrating & wandering this long, long time—crying & weeping from being joined with what is displeasing, being separated from what is pleasing—are greater than the water in the four great oceans.
sn15.3:2.14: From an inconceivable beginning comes transmigration. A beginning point is not evident, though beings hindered by ignorance and fettered by craving are transmigrating & wandering on. Long have you thus experienced stress, experienced pain, experienced loss, swelling the cemeteries—
sn15.3:2.15: enough to become disenchanted with all fabricated things, enough to become dispassionate, enough to be released.”
